title: [Feature]: Evaluate a MariaDB sink
problem: OCI GoldenGate lists MariaDB and Amazon RDS for MariaDB as supported MySQL-family connections. nats-sinks has an Oracle MySQL sink backlog item, but MariaDB dialect and connector behavior may diverge enough to need a separate profile.
proposal: Research whether MariaDB should be certified under the future Oracle MySQL sink, a generic MySQL-compatible connector, or a separate MariaDB sink. The evaluation should compare driver support, `INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE`, JSON column behavior, transaction semantics, TLS, and cloud-hosted variants.
users: MariaDB operators and managed MariaDB users who need safe JetStream event persistence.
delivery_semantics: Success may be returned only after a MariaDB transaction commits. Duplicate redelivery must use deterministic keys and documented upsert or ignore behavior.
security: Credentials, connection strings, table identifiers, and payloads must be redacted. Bind parameters and strict identifier validation are mandatory.
acceptance: Research decides whether MariaDB is a profile or separate sink.
Dialect differences from MySQL are documented.
Unit tests use fakes and no live MariaDB service.
tests: Add dialect/config tests if accepted. Gate live MariaDB tests behind explicit environment settings.
documentation: Add MariaDB findings to MySQL sink planning and roadmap documentation.
closeout: Close after research or implementation release.
